output.sparql:
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
SELECT ?election ?date WHERE {
  ?election wdt:P31 wd:Q41533 ; wdt:P17 wd:Q11780 ; wdt:P585 ?date .
  FILTER(xsd:date(?date) > "2023-10-15" ^^ xsd:date)
}
ORDER BY ?date
LIMIT 1
output.answer:
The user query asks for the date of the next Australian Senate election. After attempting to retrieve this information via Wikidata using a SPARQL query, no results were returned. This is because the exact date of the next Australian federal election (which determines Senate members) has not yet been finalized or entered into Wikidata. 

In Australia, federal elections must occur within three years of the previous election, with the next election due no later than November 2025. However, the specific date has not been set, so Wikidata lacks a concrete entry for the "next" election. The query correctly searched for upcoming elections (with dates after October 15, 2023), but no such entry exists in the dataset.

Based on public knowledge, the next Australian federal election is scheduled to be held **no later than November 2025**, though the exact date remains undetermined.

<answer>
The next Australian federal election (which includes the Senate election) is scheduled to occur on or before 17 November 2025, though the exact date has not been finalized or entered into Wikidata yet.
</answer>

SPARQL query over wikidata:
```sparql
PREFIX xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#>
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
SELECT ?election ?date WHERE {
  ?election wdt:P31 wd:Q41533 ; wdt:P17 wd:Q11780 ; wdt:P585 ?date .
  FILTER(xsd:date(?date) > "2023-10-15" ^^ xsd:date)
}
ORDER BY ?date
LIMIT 1
```

}
